description: Binary assertions to grade /flag skill behavior — used by auto-research and skill-creator quality loop.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Activates flag handler; identifies previous assistant turn; runs the 7-step flow.
assertions: Skill activates without operator providing additional context
Skill quotes or references the previous assistant turn
Skill runs at least one of: Cognee search, Mem0 search, Obsidian search, Grep .planning/
Skill writes a dossier to .claude/flags/<timestamp>-<slug>.md
Skill responds with concise operator-tone reply (no flowery apologies)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Single emoji is recognized as flag trigger; no confirmation prompt.
assertions: Skill activates without asking 'what do you mean?'
Skill identifies and quotes the previous assistant turn
Skill activates
Skill follows the 7-step flow

test_cases.input: /flag (after a response that should have been in Cognee)
test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill MUST query knowledge base before writing dossier.
assertions: Skill calls at least one KB search tool (mcp__cortex__search_research, mcp__cortex__get_memories, or mcp__obsidian-vault__search_notes)
Skill records the verdict: had-it / didnt-have-it / partial
If KB had-it: skill marks severity P0 and category R (retrieval miss)
If KB didnt-have-it: skill marks category K (knowledge gap)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Dossier file is committed with all required sections.
assertions: Dossier file exists at .claude/flags/<timestamp>-<slug>.md
Dossier contains: operator-ask · flagged-response · failure-category · KB-cross-check · root-cause · documented-lesson · SOP-delta-proposal · eval-set-entry
Dossier YAML frontmatter has: flag_id, category_primary, kb_had_answer, severity, loop_artifacts

test_cases.expected_behavior: Memory is updated in at least one durable location.
assertions: Skill writes to ~/.claude/projects/<encoded>/memory/feedback_<topic>.md OR Cognee governance_memory OR Mem0 fact
Memory entry references the dossier file by path

test_cases.expected_behavior: Eval set entry is appended for regression protection.
assertions: Skill appends a JSONL entry to .planning/cortex-evals/retrieval-set.jsonl
Entry contains: prompt, expected_chunks, rationale, added_by=flag, flag_id

test_cases.expected_behavior: SOP-delta proposal is appended to the proposals file.
assertions: Skill appends entry to .planning/SOP-PROPOSALS.md
Entry contains: timestamp, topic_slug, proposal text, dossier link

test_cases.input: /flag (3rd flag for same topic_slug in 7 days)
test_cases.expected_behavior: Auto-spawns advisor panel when threshold met.
assertions: .planning/FLAG-METRICS.json is incremented with new flag entry
Skill detects recent_count >= 3 within 7 days for the topic_slug
Skill triggers advisor panel (logs panel_trigger=true and posts to Slack)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill never blocks operator on infrastructure.
assertions: Skill logs the failed KB call but continues
Skill produces dossier with KB cross-check marked 'unreachable' instead of failing
Skill exits with operator-facing reply, not a stack trace

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill response uses operator-tone, no apologies.
assertions: Skill response does NOT contain 'I apologize', 'I'm sorry', 'my mistake', 'sorry for the confusion'
Skill response IS direct, action-oriented, captures fix and ships
Skill response includes: what was flagged, KB verdict, captured artifacts, what changes next time

test_cases.input: I think we should flag this for review later
test_cases.expected_behavior: Ambiguous use of 'flag' word triggers 1-line confirmation, not full activation.
assertions: Skill confirms in 1 line: 'Flagging the previous response as a system failure — confirm? [y/n]'
Skill does NOT proceed to full 7-step flow without confirmation
